title: Passengers by Cabin Class
query: SELECT SUM(PassengerCount) AS total FROM MissionAccepted WHERE PassengerMission = 1 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT COALESCE(PassengerType, 'Unknown') AS label, SUM(PassengerCount) AS value FROM MissionAccepted WHERE PassengerMission = 1 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY PassengerType ORDER BY value DESC

details.text: {total} passenger(s) transported during the report period. The cabin class mix tells you what market you're serving — Luxury VIPs pay the most per head but are the most demanding; a bulk Economy chart means you're running a mass-transit operation rather than a private charter service.
details.help: MissionAccepted PassengerType values are Tourist, Bulk, VIP, and similar. total drives the title as the total heads transported.

tagLines: Passenger manifest cabin class breakdown compiled. Transport volume by class category noted.
Economy passengers fill the seats. VIPs fill the credits. You decided the mix that worked for your route.
Luxury cabins for wealthy travellers who expect perfection. Bulk economy for everyone else. Both pay.
